summary: The persistent mismatch on the 'pm' output is due to incorrect toggling logic: the current RTL toggles 'pm' when the hour rolls from 12 to 1, but the correct behavior is to toggle 'pm' when the clock rolls from 11:59:59 to 12:00:00 (i.e., at the start of the 12th hour, not after it). The patch moves the pm toggle to occur exactly when the hour increments from 11:59:59 to 12:00:00, matching the reference model and fixing the sim failure.
risks: If the testbench or reference model expects a different PM toggle point (e.g., at 12:59:59 -> 1:00:00), this change could cause new mismatches.
If there are subtle BCD encoding or hour increment edge cases not covered by the testbench, they may not be detected until further testing.
next_steps: Re-run simulation to confirm that the 'pm' output now matches the reference and all benchmark tests pass.
Carefully review hour and PM transitions in the waveform to ensure correct behavior at all rollovers.
If any new mismatches appear, compare the DUT and reference model at the transition points for further debugging.
